topic: How long does it actually take your team to fully onboard one new employee?
format: State the uncomfortable truth about how most Oman businesses handle this problem — be specific and honest, not preachy
post:
Most businesses in Oman couldn't tell you exactly how long it takes to onboard one new employee. Not because they don't care — because no one has ever counted.

Here's what it actually looks like:

HR types the offer letter manually. Sends it on WhatsApp. Chases a signature for three days. Finance sets up payroll in a separate sheet. Someone else submits the work permit. MOL filings get added to a to-do list that lives in someone's head.

Two weeks later, the employee still isn't fully set up on WPS payroll — and no one is sure who's responsible for the SPF registration.

That's not a people problem. That's a systems problem.

When every step lives in a different tool — or no tool at all — onboarding one person quietly eats 6–10 hours of admin across your team. Multiply that by every hire this year. That's a lot of hours spent on work that shouldn't be this hard.

SmartPRO Hub puts contracts, WPS payroll, SPF, Ministry of Labour filings, and work permit tracking in one place. One login. One source of truth.

Onboarding stops being a fire drill.

How long does it actually take your team to onboard one person right now? Drop a number in the comments.
